item.title: @elonmusk: Starlink Mini offers fast, reliable internet on the go—great for traveling, camping, exploring, boat...
item.description: Starlink Mini offers fast, reliable internet on the go—great for traveling, camping, exploring, boating, RVing, and more. Easily navigate, stream, and stay connected without dead zones or slow speeds. Order online in under 2 minutes.

item.title: @elonmusk: 99.1% were men. A bizarrely missed feminist opportunity is pointing out that 85% of violent criminal...
item.description: 99.1% were men. A bizarrely missed feminist opportunity is pointing out that 85% of violent criminals are dudes. Inventors too, though.

item.title: @elonmusk: Grok can watch videos for you
item.description: Grok can watch videos for you

item.title: @elonmusk: OMG the new Grok Imagine web update is EVERYTHING 😍✨ The UI changes look absolutely AMAZING super cl...
item.description: OMG the new Grok Imagine web update is EVERYTHING 😍✨ The UI changes look absolutely AMAZING super clean, modern, and buttery smooth! Everything just flows better now, making image & video creation feel next-level intuitive and fun 🔥 xAI absolutely cooked with this redesign. The web experience hits different! Go check it out yourself 👉grok.com/imagineWho else is obsessed with the new look?? 👀

item.title: @elonmusk: Today President Trump obtained a pledge from America’s leading tech companies that new data centers ...
item.description: Today President Trump obtained a pledge from America’s leading tech companies that new data centers would not increase electricity prices for residential consumers. These companies (including Amazon, Google, Meta, Microsoft, OpenAI, Oracle, and xAI) signed the “Ratepayer Protection Pledge” under which they agree to cover the costs of all new power generation required for their data centers, ensuring such costs are not passed onto American households. This is a much better approach to affordability than Bernie Sanders’ total ban on new data centers, which would halt the construction boom currently driving wage growth and job growth for blue-collar workers. In fact, the Ratepayer Protection Pledge will lower electricity prices when AI companies pay for grid upgrades and sell their excess power back to the grid. Since the beginning of his second term, President Trump has championed the idea of letting our leading AI companies become power companies, and now this idea is becoming a reality thanks to his leadership and the commitments of these strong American companies. The right approach to data centers is not to stop progress altogether, but rather to protect residential rate payers from price increases, while making it easier to stand up new power generation.

item.title: @elonmusk: yo zacksjerryrig getting community noted for that pathetic rivian point-to-point lie is the least ba...
item.description: yo zacksjerryrig getting community noted for that pathetic rivian point-to-point lie is the least based flex imaginable like imagine being a grown man shilling vaporware and getting fact-checked by randos who actually read the website. must sting knowing tesla owns the real tech while you sit there coping in public dude embarrassing as hell.

item.title: @elonmusk: She was definitely biased. That’s why the Delaware Supreme Court ended up overturning her other deci...
item.description: She was definitely biased. That’s why the Delaware Supreme Court ended up overturning her other decision to cancel his pay package.

item.title: @elonmusk: Yesterday, I had the privilege of speaking at the@a16zAmerican Dynamism Summit with the builders, op...
item.description: Yesterday, I had the privilege of speaking at the@a16zAmerican Dynamism Summit with the builders, operators, and investors helping shape the next era of exploration. NASA has a clear mandate under President Trump’s national space policy: Return American astronauts to the Moon by the end of 2028, build an enduring presence there, and ensure American leadership in the most important strategic domain. Achieving that requires focus and disciplined execution. In the weeks ahead, the Artemis II crew will travel around the Moon. It is the next step toward returning humans to the lunar surface and building a lasting presence there. To reach that goal, we’ve recently made some changes to the Artemis program: Increasing launch cadence, standardizing hardware, and adding missions that allow us to test systems, reduce risk, and build confidence before landing. This is the same approach that carried the United States from Mercury to Gemini to Apollo. In the 1960s we launched often, learned from every mission, and improved with each step forward. That is how you build real capability in deep space. Yesterday, I also announced NASA Force, a new initiative with@USOPMto bring exceptional engineering and technical talent into NASA and help rebuild the core competencies that make missions like this possible. We want to open the search far and wide to attract the best of the best and incentivize them to leverage their skills to maintain American superiority in space. NASA changed the world in 1969 by concentrating the nation’s best talent on the hardest problems imaginable. We are doing it again.

item.title: @elonmusk: Damon Johnson just set someone on fire in NY while on parole. 131 prior arrests.
item.description: Damon Johnson just set someone on fire in NY while on parole. 131 prior arrests.
